mnemonics: HOR: A function is like a HORse - Every Head (input) has One Rider (output).
OTO Cat: A One-TO-One function is like a picky cat - It Only Takes One (OTO) kind of treat (output) per owner (input).
keyPoints: A function maps every element in the domain to exactly one element in the codomain.
An injective (one-to-one) function never maps two distinct elements to the same element.
The horizontal line test can determine if a function is one-to-one from its graph.
commonMistakes: Confusing a plain function with being one-to-one; all one-to-one functions are functions, but not all functions are one-to-one.
Assuming a function must be onto (surjective) to be one-to-one; injectivity and surjectivity are independent properties.
